{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Alekhine Defence : Krejcik Variation| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | |bl| |nd| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl| |nl|rl|= || }} = Alekhine Defence: Krejcik Variation = White's only way to recover the pawn back is [[/3. Bxf7/|3. Bxf7+]]. This move may seem like a horrible blunder that loses a bishop, but in fact it wins a knight instead. This move also prevents the king from castling, but the black king will be in a relatively safe position after 3... Kxf7 4. Qh5+ Kg8 5. Qd5+ e6 6. Qxe4 d5! ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}}. :'1.e4 Nf6 2. Bc4 Nxe4'
